title: Abutilon grewiifolium
taxonomy: Abutilon grewiifolium is classified within the Malvaceae family, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as mallow or hibiscus family. This family comprises around 243 genera and 4,330 species, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Abutilon itself is native to the Americas and contains around 100 species, most of which are known for their bright flowers and ability to thrive in warm, sunny environments. Abutilon grewiifolium is specifically native to South America, where it grows as a shrub or small tree. The species is often cultivated for its ornamental value, particularly for its bright yellow flowers that bloom throughout the year.

intro: Abutilon grewiifolium is a medicinal plant species within the Malvaceae family, commonly known as the hollyhock family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits.
